political.s: Callaway Golf has minimal federal political activity with no reported lobbying expenses and only $800 in contributions in 2018. The company maintains a neutral stance on political issues.

charity.s: The Callaway Golf Company Foundation has donated over $14 million to charities since inception, focusing on youth development, military support, health and wellness, and disability advocacy. The foundation provides employee matching gifts and a dollars-for-doers program for volunteer initiatives.
charity.amt: Over $14 million cumulative; ongoing annual grants
charity.focus: Youth development, military/veterans support, health and wellness, disability advocacy, golf accessibility

environment.s: Callaway has published sustainability reports since 2022 and implemented initiatives including renewable energy consumption at 60% at headquarters, recycled materials in products, water management systems, and supplier auditing through SMETA protocol. The company achieved no product recalls due to environmental compliance in 2021.

labor.s: Callaway emphasizes workforce diversity with over 50% of management promotions and new hires being women since 2019. The company employs a Director of Diversity, Equity and Inclusion, ranks in top 5% for diversity on Comparably, and maintains comprehensive employee benefits and training programs.

dei.s: Callaway demonstrates strong pro-DEI commitment with a dedicated DEI leadership role, sustainability strategy prioritizing diversity and inclusion, and public initiatives promoting golf accessibility for underrepresented groups. The company reports over 50% of management positions filled by women since 2019.

animals.s: N/A - Callaway Golf manufactures golf equipment and apparel and does not engage in animal testing.

guns.s: N/A - Callaway Golf manufactures golf equipment, golf balls, apparel, and golf accessories. The company does not manufacture or sell firearms.

privacy.s: Callaway Golf provides privacy policies for its website and digital services but limited public information available about comprehensive data privacy practices or third-party privacy assessments.

execPay.s: CEO Chip Brewer received approximately $9.4 million in 2024 compensation (base salary of $1.1M plus $8.2M in stock awards). Average employee salary is approximately $118,000, suggesting an estimated pay ratio of approximately 79:1 or higher.

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
